title: Compact ~/.claude/memory/.docc/MEMORY.md from ~43KB back under the 24.4KB soft limit
description: MEMORY.md is the canonical Claude auto-memory index loaded at conversation start. System reminders flagged it at the 24.4KB soft limit; current size is ~43KB after this session's 7 new entries. Many existing entries exceed the 200-char one-liner budget — they contain detail that should live in the topic file, not the index. Compaction pass: walk every line, condense over-long entries to <150 chars with detail moved into the topic file, possibly group by prefix (project_/feedback_/reference_/insights/) into sections, drop duplicate or stale pointers. Goal: index that fits in the conversation's pre-load budget so future sessions get the whole map, not a truncated prefix. Same structural pattern as `workspace-validate` for the substrate xcworkspace — both started small, accumulated, need periodic compaction. Could ship as a tool: `claude-memory-compact` Swift CLI that walks the index + applies the compaction rules. Or a manual one-time pass.
